design_philosophy.core_loop: Platforming progression through horror-themed levels with axe combat and resource management (ammo, health growth via enemy defeats)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing limited shotgun ammo, navigating hazardous platforming sections, and health bar progression through enemy attrition
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a resurrected Rick, navigating through comical super-deformed horror film parodies to rescue Jennifer from the Pumpkin King
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberate tonal shift from arcade's serious horror to cartoonish parody with super-deformed art; frames itself as a movie/dream sequence with meta narrative reveal; includes multiple horror genre homages (Thriller dance Dracula, Michael Jackson vampire, chestburster boss). Password system enables non-linear stage access rather than forced linearity.

difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with password system allowing mid-game entry
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Password system provides stage select capability; health bar growth mechanic allows players to grind enemy defeats for stat progression

progression.upgrade_system: Experience point system where defeating enemies increases Rick's health bar; threshold for growth displayed on-screen
progression.unlock_mechanism: Four-digit password system enables direct stage access; hidden crystal orbs unlock extended ending epilogue
replayability_hooks: Two collectible crystal balls that unlock extended ending sequences
Password system enables non-sequential stage exploration
Secret Japan stage accessible via passwords
Secret Egypt stage described as 'infuriating' with unique mechanics (trapdoors)

world_building_techniques: Opening cutscene: Jennifer weeping over Rick's grave, lightning revival, kidnapping by Pumpkin King
Meta-narrative twist: entire game revealed as movie within a movie; director congratulates Rick on acting
Post-credits reveal: mask comes to life, suggesting deeper threat
Extended ending (crystal ball 1): Rick and Jennifer on hill in happiness
Extended ending (crystal ball 2): Ominous approach to West Mansion with cryptic text suggesting prequel status
Stage intros establish location/mood (cabin interiors, devil town, hell house progression)

boss_design.philosophy: Parody-based horror boss encounters featuring pop culture references and film homages; health progression through stage completion affects boss difficulty scaling
notable_bosses: Michael Jackson-inspired vampire (Level 1 boss) - performs Thriller dance
Bathroom enemy (Stage 7) - appears in multiple contexts
Egyptian princess (secret stage) - grants crystal ball reward

faction_indicators: Jennifer as motivating objective (damsel archetype subverted through dream narrative)
danger_signals: Pumpkin King as orchestrating force of kidnapping and antagonism
Animated mask at game's end suggesting possessed/corrupted entity
West Mansion as ominous location in extended ending
Cultist death sequence in Stage 7 foreshadowing
environmental_storytelling: Progression through thematic spaces: mansion interior → bathroom → sewer → devil town → forest cabins → hell house
Chainsaw rooms appearing multiple times (Stage 3, Stage 7)
Japanese castle and Egyptian pyramid as exotic secret stages
Rick on hill with Jennifer vs. uncertain approach to West Mansion (visual storytelling of fate)

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Splatterhouse: Wanpaku Graffiti (debug data, stage structure, boss references)
Wikipedia - Splatterhouse: Wanpaku Graffiti (gameplay mechanics, plot, design philosophy, reception)
